finding: `validate:standard` currently offers all-or-nothing serial/parallel execution. Running the full standard profile with `--parallel --validator-timeout-ms 180000` produced 7 apparent failures/timeouts, but the same resource-heavy validators passed when run in isolation or serial lanes. This creates false red gates and hides the real distinction between functional failure, timeout, and resource contention.
expectedBehavior: The validator runner should use declared scheduler metadata such as `parallelSafe`, `executionMode`, `resourceProfile`, `resourceLocks`, `expectedDurationMs`, and per-validator timeout. Parallel-safe validators should run concurrently, while validators sharing global resources such as `.atm/runtime`, git hooks, git index, emergency audit, temp worktree bootstrap, runner cache, or release artifacts should be serialized into compatible lanes. Summaries should classify functional failure, timeout, scheduler/resource contention, and flaky isolation needs separately.
evidenceOrRepro: `node --strip-types scripts/run-validators.ts standard --parallel --validator-timeout-ms 180000 --json --run-id plan3-final-standard-diagnostic` produced total=86, passed=79, failed=7 in 188303ms. Failed/timeboxed validators included validate-task-import, validate-task-ledger-governance, validate-git-head-evidence, validate-git-hooks-enforcement, pre-team-foundation-gate, validate-examples, and validate-test-facade. Follow-up isolated runs passed: validate-task-import 99.2s, validate-task-ledger-governance 124.2s, validate-git-head-evidence 88.4s, pre-team-foundation-gate 54.7s, validate-test-facade 133.1s, validate-git-hooks-enforcement 269.6s, validate-examples 71.7s.
followUp: Resolved by adding resource-aware validator scheduling metadata and lane execution to `scripts/run-validators/implementation.ts` / `scripts/validators.config.json`: standard now defaults to bounded resource-aware parallelism with `schedulerDefaults.maxParallel`, `parallelSafe`, `executionMode`, `resourceProfile`, and `resourceLocks`, while `--serial` remains available as an escape hatch. Verification: `node --strip-types scripts/run-validators.ts standard --filter validate-task-import --filter validate-task-ledger-governance --filter validate-git-head-evidence --filter validate-git-hooks-enforcement --filter pre-team-foundation-gate --filter validate-examples --filter validate-test-facade --json --run-id scheduler-smoke-7-contention` passed 7/7 in 407593ms; `npm run validate:standard -- --json --run-id plan3-resource-aware-standard-compact` passed 86/86 in 762219ms; `node --strip-types scripts/run-validators.ts standard --filter validate-skew-matrix --json --run-id validator-resource-profile-smoke` emitted `resourceProfile=release-mirror`, `resourceLocks=[release-mirrors]`, and `schedulerLane=serial-required`.
